methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Cuby Technologies is a Series A stage company in Housing & Construction founded by Aleks Gampel and Aleh Kandrashou. The company has raised $23.4M to date. The Housing & Construction sector includes four relevant private peers (ICON, Inversion Space, Cobod, Material Evolution) but no public comparables; however, none of these peers meet the criteria for reliable peer-median triangulation due to stage and valuation dispersion.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and constitutes the sole reliable quantitative anchor. Using $23.4M in total raised as a base valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression yields an implied valuation of $47M. This method reflects the expected value step between Series A and subsequent financing rounds within the sector.

The triangulation range is bounded at $40M (low), $47M (median), and $54M (high), derived from ±15% variance around the stage-progression result. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the applicability of standard 2.0x multipliers to frontier construction technology—where market validation timelines and capital intensity may differ materially from median venture benchmarks—and the absence of direct peer comparison data to calibrate stage-progression assumptions. Additional data on comparable Series A → Series B outcomes within construction-tech would materially tighten this range.
